# Transcrição: 20-How to set up Retargeting 

- **Fonte:** 20-How to set up Retargeting 
- **Duração:** 06:26
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

Alright, so the reality is that most of your sales, if you're working with a low-ticket coaching funnel, are going to come from retargeting. And likewise, if you're working with a high-ticket funnel that's leading to a sales call, you will pick up a lot of cheaper leads through retargeting. So it's really important that we get this up and going once we have our funnels set up and tracking. Now if you don't have previous campaigns that we're running in the ad account, meaning you don't have a custom audience of people that opted in to retarget, it's probably better to set up the funnel first, all your top of funnel activities, and then set up the retargeting after that. If the client is already getting a lot of organic traffic and a lot of leads through YouTube or AdWords, it's an idea to set up the retargeting as soon as possible because they will have those audiences in there already. So I'm going to be taking you through extending the retargeting windows in what we call long-tail funnels later on, but for now we're going to set up basic retargeting. Okay, so for this kind of funnel, really we have two main custom audiences that we're going to create to retarget. The first one is going to be 14 days custom audience of people that opted in. So this could have been people that opted into our webinar or our lead magnet. And then the second audience is a five-day window of people that viewed our sales page or added to cart if you are in a low-ticket offer. And this is basically the basic setup for retargeting for this kind of funnel. So for example, the 14-day opt-ins, we're going to create this custom audience of people that opted in in the last 14 days. And we're going to make sure that we exclude leads or people that already saw the sales page because if they saw the sales page, they're actually going to be in the second retargeting window, which is the custom audience of people that viewed it in the last five days. Now some rule of thumbs here. If your custom audiences are under 500, so if you have fewer than 500 people in your custom audiences, and this is going to be hard to tell because they don't show you this in the audience tab. So you kind of have to go based off how many clicks or opt-ins you have had at the top of the funnel. But if you suspect it's under 500 people, set this up as a post-engagement objective. So set up your retargeting as a post-engagement objective campaign. It will feed your budget out a lot better than a conversion-based objective. So once I'm inside the ad account, this is pretty much the setup. I click on create and I'm going to select on engagement because I know this is a really small audience. I haven't had 500 people opt-in in the last 14 days.

## [03:00 - 06:05]

So I'm going to give this my naming conventions so that I can easily identify this. It's stage two here. Okay, and then my ad said I'm going to name it what my audience is going to be and my placements. So here is 14 day opt-ins and all placements. When you're retargeting, it's fine to use all placements because it's a small audience. They're warm. They engage with you so placements don't play as much of a role in traffic quality as they do when you run them top of funnel. So here I'm going to basically create my custom audience and I'm going to select website traffic and I'm going to select people that opted in. So opt-in. This is my custom event that I created. I'm going to click 14 days. So opted in 14 days. Now we want to make sure that we are excluding people that already purchased. So I'm going to click on custom audience, website traffic, purchase. I'm going to do 180 days here. Purchased 180 days. And then I basically click on this drop down here and click on exclude audience. So now I'm targeting people that opted in the last 14 days, but I've excluded people that have purchased. I'll also create a second audience of people that visited the sales page in the last 180 days and exclude them from here as well. And here I'm going to just remove this because this isn't applicable to me. So this is basically how you would set up the audiences for your retargeting windows. So I've got this ad, this ad set here, retargeting people that opted in in the last 14 days. And then I'll create a second ad set of people that visited my sales page or added to cart in the last five days. Now what tends to work well here with retargeting is your, this ad set here of people that opted in in the last 14 days, you really want to send them to the sales page or the offer page or the page where they book a call. So send them there. You know, pitch the product. You can almost use probably the copy you used in your first round of rapid fire testing, but you want to ultimately send them to that sales page. With your audience of people that visited the sales page in the last five days or added to cart, what tends to work well here is urgency. So urgency or scarcity. So spaces are selling out or, you know, closing down soon.

## [06:05 - 06:26]

You can also use testimonials here and another great strategy is to annihilate objections. So if people visited the sales page, they haven't purchased yet addressing their objections in an ad is a great way to pull them back in. So this is an overview of the basic setup that you're looking at for these kinds of funnels with your retargeting.
